title: Refactor if-ladder to function heads with guards on numeric bounds
prompt:
Given:

```
def rating(score) do
  if score >= 90 do
    :excellent
  else
    if score >= 70 do
      :good
    else
      if score >= 50 do
        :fair
      else
        :poor
      end
    end
  end
end
```

Rewrite as multi-clause function heads. Use guards `when is_number(score) and score >= 90`, etc. Order matters — Elixir checks heads top-to-bottom. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Rating do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Multi-step; ordering matters. Classic textbook example from Nick Janetakis.
research_citation: Nick Janetakis: 'Refactoring Elixir Code: If, Cond and Pattern Matching'
